privacy: No compliance violations found. The document already uses the approved sole-trader wording ('SettlePay is a trading name of Finlay Salisbury, a sole trader') with no 'Ltd', company number, Companies House, or 'registered in England & Wales' claims.

FCA wording is correct and uses the approved phrasing: 'Payments are processed by these FCA-regulated partners; SettlePay is not FCA authorised or regulated.' No claim that SettlePay itself is FCA authorised/regulated.

PCI DSS wording matches the approved phrasing verbatim, including the SAQ A reference and the client's continuing responsibility to complete their own PCI Self-Assessment Questionnaire. No claim that SettlePay is 'PCI DSS compliant'.

No FSCS protection badge or implication. The no-funds-held position is stated clearly: 'We never hold, touch or control your funds or your customers' funds' and money settles directly from the PSP to the client's own bank account.

ICO registration number correctly uses the placeholder token [ICO_REGISTRATION_NUMBER]; no fabricated number. ICO address (Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow, Cheshire, SK9 5AF) and https://ico.org.uk are present in the complaints section.
No VAT number shown (correct — the business is not VAT registered). No fabricated statistics, metrics, fake urgency or countdowns.

All mandatory Privacy Policy clauses are present: controller identity & contact, categories of data, UK GDPR Art.6 lawful bases (in a clear table), purposes, third-party processors/recipients, international transfers (adequacy / IDTA / SCCs + Addendum), retention periods, full data-subject rights, how to exercise rights (one-month response, identity check, fee/refusal for unfounded requests), and the right to complain to the ICO.
Correct 'Last updated: 7 June 2026' date at the top.

UK English and plain language used throughout (organise/recognise spellings; solo-founder 'we/you' voice; no emoji; Title Case headings). The word 'partners' in 'FCA-regulated partners' is permitted by the approved phrasing and does not overstate a partnership.
Minor non-blocking polish applied only: contact-email link consistency and a small clarity tweak to the PSP-processor sentence. No substantive legal change.

Note (operational, not a document defect): the policy asserts a 'written data-processing agreement' with each processor — ensure this is actually in place for each provider used, and replace [ICO_REGISTRATION_NUMBER] with the real number once ICO registration is confirmed.
cookies: No compliance defects found. The document correctly identifies SettlePay as 'a trading name of Finlay Salisbury, a sole trader' and explicitly states 'it is not a limited company'. No company number, Companies House registration, or 'Ltd'/'Limited' claims appear.

No prohibited financial-promotion language: there is no 'FCA authorised/regulated' claim, no 'PCI DSS compliant' claim, no FSCS badge, and no 'Powered by' partnership overstatement (none would be expected in a Cookie Policy, and none are present).

No fabricated numbers, dates, or statistics. The ICO registration number correctly uses the placeholder token [ICO_REGISTRATION_NUMBER] rather than a fabricated number. No VAT number is shown (correct, as the business is not VAT registered).

All mandatory Cookie Policy clauses are present: PECR legal basis (with the strictly-necessary consent exemption correctly explained), cookie categories table, consent before non-essential cookies, how to manage/refuse cookies via browser settings, similar technologies (local/session storage, pixels), third-party CDN font disclosure, consequences of disabling, future-changes process, contact details, and the right to complain to the ICO with its full address and website.
Voice and style are compliant: UK English ('personalise'), 'we'/'you' (no 'our team'), Title Case headings with sentence-case body, no emoji, no fake urgency or fabricated metrics.
Controller identity and contact (hello@settlepay.uk, postal address, enquiry form) and the cross-reference to the Privacy Policy are correctly included.

Minor non-blocking observations (not defects, no change required): the document is honest that the site sets only strictly-necessary cookies/storage and runs no analytics or tracking; it correctly notes no consent banner is needed currently and commits to showing one before any non-essential cookies are set. This is consistent with PECR and the stated business facts. The corrected Markdown is returned unchanged because the document is already fully compliant.
terms: Minor (fixed): UK English verb/noun spelling of 'license/licence'. In UK English the verb is spelt 'license' (with an 's') and the noun 'licence' (with a 'c'). Section 7 used the noun spelling 'licence' as a verb in 'we assign or licence to you the rights' and 'we may describe... '. Corrected the verb usages to 'license' while leaving noun usages ('a licence', 'your assignment or licence of the Deliverables', 'licensed under their own terms') as 'licence'/'licensed'.

No substantive compliance defects found. The document correctly: (1) describes SettlePay as 'a trading name of Finlay Salisbury, a sole trader' and expressly states it is NOT a limited company with no company number / no Companies House registration / no Ltd (Sections 1, 2, 17 and closing paragraph); (2) states SettlePay is NOT FCA authorised/regulated and that payment processing is provided by the client's chosen FCA-regulated PSP (Section 4.1); (3) uses the approved PCI DSS phrasing, placing PCI compliance on the processor and the SAQ on the client (Section 5); (4) contains the prominent 'we never hold/receive/control funds; money settles directly from PSP to your own bank account' statement (Section 4.1); (5) contains no fabricated company number, no VAT number (states correctly 'not VAT registered'), no FSCS badge, no 'Powered by' partnership language, and explicitly disclaims agency/partnership (Sections 8 and closing); (6) uses the ICO placeholder token [ICO_REGISTRATION_NUMBER] and the correct ICO address and website (Section 17); (7) labels Lockdales Auctioneers as the live client and Harbourside Lettings as an illustrative, non-client example (Section 9); (8) carries the correct 'Last updated: 7 June 2026'; (9) sets governing law/jurisdiction as England and Wales (Section 16).

All mandatory clauses for a Terms document are present: service description (Section 4), no-funds-held statement (Section 4.1), third-party PSP responsibility (Sections 4.1, 8, 10), client obligations including their own PCI SAQ (Section 5), fees/quotes incl. no-VAT note (Section 6), IP ownership (Section 7), warranties and limitation of liability (Sections 9, 10), indemnity and confidentiality (Sections 11, 12), termination and survival (Section 13), website acceptable-use and disclaimer (Section 14), changes mechanism (Section 15), governing law England & Wales (Section 16), and complaints route incl. ICO (Section 17). Consumer statutory rights are preserved (Sections 9, 10).
